cards.title: Neurodivergent Parents: Supporting the Undiagnosed Generation
cards.body: Up to 97% of autistic people over 60 remain undiagnosed, representing a neglected generation. Experts provide guidance for family members on how to better support neurodivergent parents.

cards.title: Blended Families: Navigating New Relationships After Divorce
cards.body: When a parent remarries, adult children must balance fairness with relationship preservation. Experts discuss how to approach these sensitive family dynamics.

cards.title: Parental Anxiety: Coping When Worry Takes Over
cards.body: Parents often struggle with anticipatory anxiety during family activities, fearing worst-case scenarios. Learning to manage worry helps you model resilience for your children.

cards.title: Only Children Aren't Lonely or Maladjusted
cards.body: Persistent myths portray only children as selfish and lonely, but evidence shows they thrive. Parents choosing to have one child shouldn't face judgment.

cards.title: Childhood Trauma: Breaking Cycles in Adult Relationships
cards.body: Adults raised by narcissistic or avoidant parents often struggle with healthy relationship patterns. Recognizing these patterns is essential to building functional partnerships.

cards.title: Bright Children Without Tutors: Supporting Academic Potential
cards.body: Parents worry gifted children need expensive tutors to succeed academically. Your own support and encouragement may matter more than professional coaching.

cards.title: Parenting Through Medical Misinformation and Health Conspiracy Theories
cards.body: When loved ones pursue risky health trends rooted in conspiracy theories, family interventions become necessary. Understanding the psychology behind these beliefs helps families respond effectively.

cards.title: Family Resilience: Finding Strength Through Mindfulness
cards.body: Mindful awareness helps parents accept that family life involves inevitable challenges and changes. Asking yourself whether worries are true helps build perspective and resilience.

cards.title: Father Wounds: The Impact of Emotionally Absent Dads
cards.body: Not all father wounds stem from physical absence; some come from fathers emotionally unreachable despite being present. Recognizing these patterns helps adult children heal.

cards.title: Millennial Couples: Why Shared Parenting Still Causes Conflict
cards.body: Modern couples value equality and shared parenting, yet many still struggle with execution. Therapists identify preparation gaps that leave millennials unprepared for this reality.

cards.title: Queer Youth: Healing from Childhood Trauma and Safety
cards.body: Damaging childhood experiences can leave lasting wounds for queer people, even after coming out as adults. Examining and healing these early wounds is crucial for wellbeing.

cards.title: Young Adults Living With Parents: Making It Work
cards.body: Rising costs force more young adults back home with their parents. Practical strategies can help families navigate shared living and maintain healthy boundaries.
